comment.body:
I want to invite you to an APRIL CHALLENEGE
Let's be more conscious with the money.
It is not financial advise, it is just a game ;))

Wealthy plp invest the majority of their savings rather than spending on luxury goods that serve as status symbols. 
They focus on assets that appreciate over time—stocks, real estate, businesses, or diversified index funds.
Tax-deferred retirement accounts, strategic asset allocation, and sometimes even trusts, keeping a low profile in spending minimizes lifestyle inflation that can attract unnecessary tax or social pressures.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vOhMhri31WM :))))

The key is: 
Income = savings + Necessary expenses + discretionary spending
Determine your target savings rate and stick to it. If your expenses exceed your targets, adjust either by cutting non-essential costs or recalculating your percentages.

1) Monitor your YoY monthly spendings, and calculate the income/spending ratio % by cathegories: necessities (actual necessities, like housing, food, transportation, and the necessary amount only! don't cheat yourself), discretionary spendings, savings, and investments (including trading), and check the "left-tail" spendings in avg and its SD to check the sudden and necessary spendings (e.g. fire, hospital, broken car, etc.)
And adjust inflation

2) Lets try “safe withdrawal rate” of your accumulated net worth. The idea is to have enough assets so that even if you stop earning an income, your lifestyle can be maintained by withdrawing only a safe percentage each year.
Lets say your monthly expenses is 30% of your monthly income, you can calculate the annual expenses. 
Then divide it by 0.04 (as the commmon safe withdrawal rate is 4%), and this will give you the required net worth, in case you  wish to eventually fund your lifestyle solely from your investments. 

3) Using these datas, lets use budgeting rules (like a modified 50/30/20 or even more aggressive splits) that break down expenses into fixed needs, discretionary spending, and savings + investments. 
For example, with 70% saved: 
- necessities 25% of your monthly income
- discretionary spendings (5% of your income)
The rest is saving. And based on the historical data, you separate some amount for emergency (based on the historical SD upper lvls). 

4) For trading, always have a monthly amount of money that you put into work in the market. And calculate the daily amounts. 
Have a min profit target and a max loss target, and before trading, always have a plan: decide what strategy (long, short, directional or premium collecting, etc.) you will do based on the market state, what is your target entry, what signs you are looking for to prove or disprove you, and what is the betsize you are willing to give for that bet. And then lets be sticked to it.
Never deviate.
With this, you can make a safe and steady growth.
Read my post about risk managemnet.

Lets do this challenge into April as you wish, 
make financial plans for the next month, its weeks, and its days,
And lets recheck next EOM, how it was going. :))

comment.body:
You make an excellent point! I think Warren Buffett, despite being a financial genius, seems to have fallen into the classic trap of wealth accumulation that can sometimes miss the deeper psychological aspects of money's utility.
I see money mainly as a tool be being free. However, once you reach to this stage, we have to take into account the non-linear utility of money. Essentially, the marginal value of each additional dollar decreases as your wealth increases. The difference between $0 and $10,000 is life-changing. The difference between $10 million and $11 million? Not so much.

comment.body:
SPX Game Plan – Friday, March 28
 JPM Collar Resets Monday: 5565/4700 P-Spd Long, 6165 C Short ~39k

CONTEXT
• Thursday = tariff reaction → LOD 5670 / HOD 5730 / Close 5693
• SPX filled Monday gap-up and reclaimed 5665 overnight
• Semis/AI unwind pressured broader indices
• Key data: PCE @ 8:30 / Sentiment @ 10:00
• EM = ±42 → 5640–5725
• 5700 = straddle magnet w/ large p/c OI
• Charm builds below 5600
• 5565 = JPM collar put floor

KEY STRUCTURE
 Put OI: 5750 ITM, clusters 5720–5710, 5650, 5610
 Call OI: 5650–75 fly, 5700 large, stack 5725–5750+
SPX ROAD MAP – 0DTE

CHOP ZONE: 5670–5700
• IF SPX holds 5670–5700 → chop zone dominates
• Mixed OI, no strong charm = indecision
• >5670 <5700 → weak drift higher
• <5700 >5670 → float zone, low flow
• Hold here midday = likely 5700 pin

ABOVE CHOP ZONE
• >5700 → opens 5711–5715
• >5715 → path to 5730–5735
• >5735 → breakout to 5747.75
• >5748 → grind to 5765–5770 gamma flip
⚠️ 5770+ = stabilizing zone, upside fades out
BELOW CHOP ZONE
• <5670 → opens 5650 → 5630
• <5630 → speed pocket to 5610–5600
• <5600 → charm builds aggressively → target 5565
⚠️ 5565–5550 = structural base, potential reversal band
RECLAIM LOGIC
• <5650 → reclaim >5670 → bounce to 5700
• <5630 → reclaim 5650 → range re-engages
• >5735 → fail <5748 → fade into 5715/5700
• <5610 → reclaim 5630 → reflexive rally to 5650/70
EXTREME SCENARIOS (~±60 pts from 5670)
• >5748 → grind to 5765/5770
⚠️ 5770+ = slow, exhausted structure
• <5610 → fast drop to 5565
⚠️ Dealer bid builds → 5565–5550 = reversal band

comment.body:
Probability did not favour for a bounce at all (always my read not necessarily right.) I’ll tell you why for me.
Positive charm makes MM longer delta, as we approach the 5600 area where gamma started to increase, this gave us the info that there, Gamma makes vol goes down ( this choppy PA we’ve seen for 1h 30min) and as we are in negative Vanna we know that if vol goes down MM becomes longer delta as well. So this is why it’s wrong to look at gamma as support or resistance level. In this case I think that gamma slowed the price there only increasing the prob to go further lower and suppressive. Hope it makes sense (let me know what you think 👍🏻)
I got out my long just I realised this mechanism that would not be favour for what I bet on.

comment.body:
You're right about the repelling effect. Let me explain it correctly:

For SPX at 5580 with 40,000 customer long/dealer short puts at 5565 expiring in 3 days, the strike acts as a repellent rather than a pin.

When dealers are short puts at 5565, their hedging behavior creates a repelling force:

- If SPX moves down toward 5565, dealers must sell more futures/stocks to hedge (becoming shorter delta as puts gain delta), pushing the price even lower and away from the strike
- If SPX moves up away from 5565, dealers buy back their hedges (reducing short exposure as puts lose delta), accelerating the upward move

This creates a "repelling" effect around large put positions, where price movement tends to accelerate away from the strike rather than pin to it. The large gamma exposure at 5565 acts as an "anti-pin" or repellent point.

The market can appear to "bounce off" these levels rather than cluster around them, especially with very large positions that create significant dealer gamma imbalances.

comment.body:
Here's a strat you all may like in high vol times: butterflies. When vol is high, butterflies get pretty cheap because they have to account for the increased risk that price moves all the way through it to the other side. This lets you get pretty big ranges - like 20pts a side (40 wide), for what would normally be the cost of a 20-wide.
Then, if towards the end of day price is above/below the range, you can often naked call/put in between. So you do a 10x butterfly and a 1x call/put. If the price moves against your naked call/put, it then moves into a gigantic profit pool. You just have to hope it doesn't keep moving all the way through it.
Not finance advice, but something to consider
